steps.title: Identify the equation form
steps.content: Each increase of 1 in $x$ multiplies $y$ by 3, so this is exponential growth: $y = a \cdot b^x$, where $a$ is the value when $x = 0$ and $b$ is the growth factor. Here $a = 200$ and $b = 3$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 200(x)^3$: This is a power function (polynomial), not exponential — $x$ is the base, not the exponent, so increasing $x$ by 1 does not multiply $y$ by a constant factor. ✗
  • Choice B — $y = 200(3)^x$: When $x = 0$, $y = 200$ ✓; when $x$ increases by 1, $y$ is multiplied by 3 ✓. Matches all conditions. ✓
  • Choice C — $y = 3(x)^{200}$: Power function with wrong initial value ($y = 0$ when $x = 0$) and 3 is the coefficient, not the growth base. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 3(200)^x$: Exponential, but the initial value when $x = 0$ is 3, not 200, and each step multiplies by 200, not 3. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
